description: Small deterministic, non-private prompt set created for reproducible router-trace capture.
privacy: Contains no personal, confidential, credential, or user-vault data.

prompts.text: A security analyst compares a suspicious domain with trusted threat intelligence and records the evidence for review.
The incident report lists a phishing attachment, a command interpreter, and a credential access attempt.
A retrieval system should cite the source passage that supports each technical claim.
An electronics student calculates a timer frequency, builds the circuit, and measures the waveform with an oscilloscope.
A signed integer multiply accumulator is checked against a bit exact software reference model.
The design report separates simulation results from synthesis estimates and physical measurements.
A marine scientist records temperature, depth, location, and uncertainty before interpreting an observation.
A controlled experiment changes one parameter at a time and repeats the measurement with the same procedure.
A deterministic test uses fixed inputs, checks explicit invariants, and reports a reproducible failure.
A safe local tool rejects path traversal, limits input size, and avoids overwriting existing files.
